channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: EXL’s Insurance LLM transforms claims and underwriting
item.description: As insurance companies embrace generative AI (genAI) to address longstanding operational inefficiencies, they’re discovering that general-purpose large language models (LLMs) often fall short in solving their unique challenges.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 11’s Start menu search gets new, clearer labels, as Microsoft tries to avoid EU regulation trouble
item.description: Microsoft is updating Windows 11’s Start menu search in the EEA (European Economic Area) to comply with EU rules, separating on-device and Bing web search while adding more user control options.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: AI and scientists unite to decipher old scrolls charred by the Vesuvius volcano
item.description: UK scientists have announced what they term a “historic breakthrough,” after the first image of the inside of a scroll carbonized by a volcanic eruption 2,000 years ago was uncovered.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless And Pan Pylas, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Warren finds common ground with Trump over debanking concerns
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) seems to have found common ground with President Trump and Republican lawmakers over “debanking” concerns, urging the president Tuesday to take action to prevent Americans from being improperly locked out of the financial system.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Fast fashion, laptops and toys are likely to cost more due to US tariffs on Chinese imports
item.description: A sweeping new U.S. tariff on products made in China is expected to increase the prices American consumers pay for a wide array of products, from the ultra-cheap apparel sold on online shopping platforms to toys and electronic devices such as computers and cellphones.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The Switch 2 Nintendo Direct now has official times for its April 2 broadcast
item.description: We now have an official time for the April 2 Nintendo Switch 2 Direct, and it's probably exactly what you expected.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop navigating the cloud, and start shaping it
item.description: Digital transformation is a journey that organizations embark on to integrate digital technologies into all aspects of their operations.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: China targets Apple’s business practices; maybe the company should adapt
item.description: They say great art reflects its time. When it comes to Apple’s business, just as the iMac and iPod matched the zeitgeist, the company must now develop its business to meet the complex demands of a divided, post-globalist world struggling with economic and climate insecurity.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Ex-Google employee charged by US with espionage to boost AI in China
item.description: Linwei Ding, who was indicted in March, now faces seven counts of economic espionage along with seven counts of theft of trade secrets under a revised indictment.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Silicon Valley’s brilliant young techies help Musk, Trump penetrate government, drawing Democrats’ fury
item.description: Bay Area members of Congress blasted incursions by Elon Musk's team into government's sensitive data and systems.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Baron, The Mercury News

item.title: Google opens door to AI-powered weapons, surveillance
item.description: The company that once opened its code of conduct with the words “Don’t be evil” is now opening the door to assisting in the creation of AI-powered weapons and surveillance systems.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Google Pixel 9a freebies have seemingly leaked, but there’s one notable omission
item.description: The Google Pixel 9a might come with a trio of free subscriptions, but could miss out on an AI upgrade.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: DeepSeek's rise raises data privacy, national security concerns
item.description: The emergence of a newly popular artificial intelligence (AI) model from Chinese startup DeepSeek is raising national security and data privacy concerns for the U.S., not unlike those that spurred a ban on TikTok last month.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Apple lashes out at iPhone porn app maker and the EU rules allowing its download
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Apple has chided a newly introduced pornography app available in the European Union, warning that the bloc’s digital rules opening the way for third-party app store downloads undermines consumer confidence in the tech giant.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Así es el piloto que compara mediante IA generativa ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones
item.description: Carreras Grupo Logístico e IBM, en colaboración con Seidor, han desarrollado un proyecto piloto para comparar automáticamente ofertas de proveedores en licitaciones mediante el uso de inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Apple launches Invites, its event invitation app that takes on Partiful
item.description: Apple rolled out its newest iPhone app called Invites, which lets iCloud+ subscribers create and share invitations to anyone, regardless of whether they have an Apple account or Apple device. Hosts have control over the events.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: California State University Partners with Tech Giants to Launch AI-Powered Higher Education System
item.description: San Jose State University and CSU East Bay are among the California state universities whose students and teachers could benefit from the artificial intelligence push.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence
item.description: "I think we think of AI as primarily a bunch of the software geeks who are out there coding somewhere, but there's real hardware involved," said Neil Chilson. The post A look inside the Trump administration approach to artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Judge says Elon Musk's claims of harm from OpenAI are a 'stretch' but welcomes possible trial
item.description: Elon Musk’s lawyers faced off with OpenAI in court Tuesday as a federal judge weighed the billionaire’s request for a court order that would block the ChatGPT maker from converting itself to a for-profit company.
item.dc:creator: Barbara Ortutay And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hawley makes push to allow parents to sue tech companies
item.description: Sen. Josh Hawley (R-Mo.) said Tuesday he will introduce legislation to empower parents to sue leading social media companies over alleged harms to children and teens.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: How an ancient asteroid strike carved out 2 grand canyons on the moon
item.description: New research shows that when an asteroid slammed into the moon billions of years ago, it carved out a pair of grand canyons on the far side.
item.dc:creator: Marcia Dunn, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Adobe Introduces AI-Powered Contract Intelligence in Acrobat AI Assistant
item.description: Adobe's new contract intelligence features aim to simplify complex agreements for businesses and professionals, leveraging AI-generated summaries, data security, and compliance monitoring.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump signs order to create a sovereign wealth fund that could buy TikTok
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump signed an executive order on Monday ordering the creation of a sovereign wealth fund within the next year, saying it could potentially buy the short video app TikTok. Trump offered little in the way of detail and it was unclear how such a wealth fund would work.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: I’m a tech expert: 10 AI prompts you’ll use all the time
item.description: From proofreading and research to brainstorming and data analysis, you can use generative AI as a sidekick for just about any task you can dream of.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canada's most populous province to pause retaliatory measures as US puts tariffs on hold
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — The leader of Ontario, Canada’s most populous province, said Monday he will pause all retaliatory measures against the United States after news broke that the threat of tariffs has been put on hold for a month.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Microsoft creates a new department to review how AI affects society
item.description: As part of Microsoft’s big push into AI services, the company has created a new department called the Advanced Planning Unit (APU). Employees of the APU will, among other things, study the effects of AI on society and make recommendations about which products are worth investing resources in.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Why second-user smartphone sales are good for Apple
item.description: Apple’s iPhone business isn’t just about new product sales. The second-user and services markets need to be considered, too.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft founder Bill Gates explores the making of his internal operating system in new memoir
item.description: As he prepares to turn 70 later this year, Microsoft founder Bill Gates' new memoir explores how his childhood quirks, upbringing, friendships and experiences coalesced into shaping his internal operating system.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bill Gates shares his thoughts on vaccine backlash, Intel's woes and Google's antitrust battle
item.description: Although Microsoft founder Bill Gates sat down for an interview with The Associated Press to discuss his new memoir, “Source Code: My Beginnings,” he also shared his views on a variety of other topics, including vaccine conspiracy views about him, his thoughts on the struggles of longtime computer c
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: SoftBank and OpenAI set up joint company to push artificial intelligence services
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology giant SoftBank Group and OpenAI stepped up their AI partnership Monday, setting up a 50-50 held company called SB OpenAI Japan.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: US cybersecurity agency's future role in elections remains murky under the Trump administration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The nation’s cybersecurity agency has played a critical role in helping states shore up the defenses of their voting systems, but its election mission appears uncertain amid sustained criticism from Republicans and key figures in the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: CHRISTINA A. CASSIDY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft reports 10% quarterly profit growth as it works to show AI investments paying off
item.description: Microsoft said Wednesday that its profit for the October-December quarter grew 10% as it works to capitalize on the huge amounts of money it has spent to advance its artificial intelligence technology.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4
